As the Universal logo appears on screen, we hear Universal's theme song being sung a cappella by...
INT. KENNEDY CENTER - PRESS BOX
Our a cappella commentators, JOHN and GAIL.
Excited, Gail?
If I weren't, would I be wearing a diaper under this dress? As John speaks, we HEAR a marching band play.
Welcome back, a cappella enthusiasts!
INT. KENNEDY CENTER - STAGE - AUGUST 2014 - NIGHT
A MILITARY BAND heads off stage as THE BARDEN BELLAS get set.
I'm John Smith and sitting to my
right is Gail Abernathy-McCaddenFeinberger.
(pointing to wedding ring) This one's gonna stick, John.
Well you saved the Jew for last.
(gleefully nodding) I did, I did.
You're listening to Let's Talkappella, the world's premiere downloadable a cappella podcast.
We are coming to you live from our nation's capitol where the Barden University Bellas are about to rock the historic Kennedy Center.
The BELLAS: BECA, CHLOE, Lilly, STACIE, CYNTHIA ROSE, JESSICA, ASHLEY and a small Latina girl, FLORENCIA FUENTES (FLO). Chloe nods at Beca, who blows the pitch pipe.
THEY SAY YOU'RE A FREAK WHEN WE'RE HAVING FUN... (song continues) This performance reflects a group at the top of its game.
INT. KENNEDY CENTER - PRESS BOX - CONTINUOUS
These ladies have broken down every barrier in their path, haven't they, Gail?
So right, John. The first allfemale group to win a national title. Three-time defending champs. And now here they are performing for the President of the United States on his birthday.
REVEAL: The PRESIDENT and FIRST LADY, who sit in a private box [STOCK FOOTAGE]. SECRET SERVICE AGENTS stand guard.
Wow. What an inspiration to girls all over the country who are too ugly to be cheerleaders.
INT. KENNEDY CENTER - STAGE - CONTINUOUS
The Bellas TRANSITION TO: Pitbull's (feat. Ke$ha) "Timber."
IT'S GOING DOWN/I'M YELLING TIMBER/YOU BETTER MOVE, YOU BETTER DANCE...
WOOOOAH (TIMBER)/WOOOOAH (TIMBER)/ WOOOOAH (IT'S GOING DOWN)...
ON STAGE: Cynthia Rose steps up to rap.
THE BIGGER THEY ARE, THE HARDER
THEY FALL... (rap continues)
INT. KENNEDY CENTER - STAGE - CONTINUOUS
The ladies MASH "Timber" with "We Got the World." HALF BELLAS THEY SAY YOU'RE A FREAK WHEN WE'RE HAVING FUN...
IT'S GOING DOWN/I'M YELLING
ANGLE ON: Beca, killing it on stage.
There's Beca Mitchell, leader of the group.
Flo does some BACKFLIPS across the stage. It's impressive.
International exchange student Flo Fuentes just earned her green card!
She may need to do that flip right over the fence and back into Mexico.
I think she's Guatemalan.
Oh, none of that matters.
ON STAGE: The ladies continue their kickass performance. It's good, but a little noisy.
There is so much going on on-stage, I don't know where to look.
Couldn't agree more. Back in my day, we just sang. Maybe snapped our fingers if we were feeling frisky.
INT. KENNEDY CENTER STAGE - CONTINUOUS
The mash-up comes to an end, and they sing the last line from "America the Beautiful."

FROM SEA TO SHINING SEA! The ladies TRANSITION TO: Miley Cyrus's "Wrecking Ball." From the rafters descends... FAT AMY! She wears a sparkly leotard and sits in a harness with billowy white material flowing from her. She looks like an angel. As she descends:
I CAME IN LIKE A WRECKING BALL!/I NEVER HIT SO HARD IN LOVE!...
Wow! What a surprise!
An overweight girl dangling from the ceiling. Who hasn't had that dream?
Lots of us!

...I CAME IN LIKE A WRECKING BALL! As the song continues, each Bella grabs on to a piece of material attached to Fat Amy's harness. It looks super cool, like something out of Cirque du Soleil. As Fat Amy spins thirty feet above the stage,

DON'T YOU EVER SAY I JUST WALKED AWAY/I WILL ALWAYS WANT YOU... During a move, Fat Amy FLIPS upside down, losing control. As she tries to correct her position, her leotard rips at the seam with her back to the audience EXPOSING her BUTT CHEEKS! The audience gasps! Thrown, the Bellas stop singing. Beca looks up in shock.
Goodbye innocence.
BACK ON JOHN AND GAIL, who hold on to each other's shoulders. SHOOTING DRAFTS
She has no underwear on. We have a commando situation! There is a
commando situation on stage! ANGLE ON: A stern-looking President and First Lady.
Who is on top of this?!
INT. KENNEDY CENTER - TECH BOOTH - CONTINUOUS
TECH GUYS frantically hit buttons to try to prevent the impending situation.
INT. KENNEDY CENTER - STAGE - CONTINUOUS

Balloons are RELEASED from the rafters, "Hail to the Chief" begins playing over the PA. Upstage, an American flag UNFURLS and a big birthday cake ROLLS out on a cart. ON STAGE: The harness TURNS Fat Amy very slowly so that she's about to face the audience. Her arms are caught up in fabric and she can't cover her privates!
Take her back up already!
She's turning. She's turning -GAIL Oh my god, the children! Would somebody think of the children!
John SLAPS a hysterical Gail across the face. ON STAGE: As Fat Amy is almost squarely showing her bits to the audience,
Avert your eyes or take it all in! You need to make a choice!
Not the front! Nobody wants to see the front!

The harness comes to a stop with Fat Amy FLASHING the audience with downtown full frontal. ANGLE ON: Reactions of shocked anticipation from the Bellas, audience members, and John and Gail. "Pitch Perfect 2"
INT. CREDIT SEQUENCE - MONTAGE

A montage of clips featuring several NEWS PERSONALITIES covering "Muff-gate." Some clips feature gifs of Fat Amy, hanging exposed with her downtown pixillated. LOCAL NEWS ANCHOR Happy birthday, Mr. President. The Australian singer who calls herself
(turns to co-host) And if you're wondering what I mean by down under, Chip. I'm talking about her vagina. She showed her vagina. To the President. TODAY SHOW HOST
All eyes were on The Barden Bellas -JAKE TAPPER
Although authorities have ruled out terrorism as a motive, The Bellas claim the mishap was merely an accident. And Fat Amy issued an
(off paper) I am deeply sorry for the upset that I have caused. I feel I have already received punishment enough... in the form of silk burn.
As Fat Amy turns to show her butt burns,
It's filth. Women who sing are just another example of cultural decay due to loose morals-JOE SCARBOROUGH Not wearing underwear seems kind of intentional, don't you think?
SHOOTING DRAFTS
Yeah, you either choose to wear underwear or you don't wear underwear.
That is a decision I make everyday.
You know, until today, I thought singing a cappella was the most embarrassing thing you could do.
(to The View panel) Usually those girls were on point but last night they were off point, off key, and their clothes on the
Yeah, but I'd rather be in that limo ride home with the real President and he be like, "That shit was off the hook!"
EXT. BARDEN CAMPUS - ONE MONTH LATER - DAY
It's a new school year. We see a bustling college campus and our Bellas coming together, heading toward somewhere.
INT. DEAN OF STUDENT AFFAIRS OFFICE - ANTEROOM- SAME TIME
Chloe nervously paces in front of the Bellas.
No, but right now, the people that run the I.C.C.A's are (points inside) in there with the Dean talking about us, and they think we're a bunch of laughing stock-appellas!
(softly, re: pun) That's awful. (then) (MORE)
Look, we've won three championships. Whatever happens in there, we'll be okay. Maybe it's a sign to focus on other stuff.
What other stuff? You know, school, jobs... life.
This group is my life. I've intentionally failed Russian Lit three times just so I could still be a Bella!
Chloe drops into her chair.
This is the worst thing that's ever happened to any of us!
Flo pats Chloe's back, and in her ever-optimistic tone:
You know, before coming to Barden, I had diarrhea for seven years. (pause) But yes, this is terrible.
I don't understand. What's the big deal, anyway?
Um, you booshed-out our commanderin-chief.
Yeah, he's not supposed to see that stuff because he's Muslim.
Stacie, have you learned anything since you've been in college?
An ancient-looking SECRETARY approaches the ladies, comically slow. Then, very judge-y...
The Dean is ready for you tramps. Try not to hump the furniture.
Yikes. The Bellas file inside. INT. DEAN OF STUDENT AFFAIRS OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
The Bellas stand in two rows as if on trial. Before them, a stern-looking John and Gail sit behind a desk on either side of the DEAN.
Ladies, you have dragged the good name of Barden University into the gutter. And you've really upset these people, whose names I've already forgotten.
(sarcastic) Nice intro, Seacrest.
Do you know who we are?
You're the National Association for the Advancement of A Cappella Performers.

That's right. And we're concerned here at the N-triple A-C-P. Your little southern exposure in front of the Commander-In-Chief has irreparably damaged the entire institution of a cappella. Funding has dropped, sponsors are pulling out, Rashida Jones is denying ever being a part of it!
And the flood of complaints! (to Fat Amy) You would not believe the heavy flow caused by your vagina.
You're an adult. You know what you just said.
Unfortunately an example must be made.
It is the decision of this governing body that the Bellas are hereby suspended from ever participating in competition at the collegiate level.
A beat of shock as this new information sinks in. Then: Gasp!
(leans in, re: Lilly) Did she say something? Did the Asian say something?!
John points a fancy pen at Lilly.
'Cause I will not be mocked!
The Dean swipes the pen from John.
Hey, that's my pen. I got it at Alcatraz.
The terms of your suspension are laid out in this document. I'm sorry, but it's over.
A concerned Chloe takes the file from Gail. She opens and reads it.
Okay, can we be reasonable? Fat Amy apologized. It was an accident. Was it?
(off document, irked) You're taking us off our I.C.C.A. victory tour? Who's going to sing the national anthem at the Puppy Bowl?
You're being replaced by the European champions. (MORE)
A German group whose skill is matched only by its professionalism.
In other words, they'll keep their pants on.
So... We can't defend our title, we can't tour...
And you can't hold any auditions. We don't need your ranks to grow like a fungus.
There's nothing left. So basically The Bellas are over.
The ladies look at each other, the grim reality sinking in.
This. is. an.... (emotionally singing) ACA-LAMITYYYYYYY!
Beca puts her hand on Chloe's shoulder to comfort her.
(sympathetic) I'm sorry that this disciplinary action has shocked you, especially since all of you are seniors. But the truth is, you're just women. And you all be pregnant soon.
Off their shocked reactions, we CUT TO...
EXT. BARDEN CAMPUS - DAY
EMILY, a super-cute eccentric freshman, strolls arm-in-arm with her mother, KATHERINE.
Mom, I'm late. It's orientation. You gotta let me go. Katherine, getting emotional, won't let go of her daughter.
SHOOTING DRAFTS
I can't. I can't do it. My only daughter, leaving the nest.
Katherine leans into Emily, singing Chicago's "If You Leave Me Now." She has a beautiful voice. Emily loves it.
IF YOU LEAVE ME NOW/YOU'LL TAKE AWAY THE BIGGEST PART OF ME/UH UH UH UH NO BABY PLEASE DON'T GO... DO YOU WANT ME TO STOP BECAUSE I CAN KEEP SINGING...?
I don't know how I'm going to get by without your spontaneous singing.
They come to a stop outside Barden's oldest building which has a banner above it: "WELCOME BARDEN CLASS OF 2018."

You're gonna love this place. Just promise me you'll call. (getting worked up) Oh and honey, don't be afraid to express yourself. But not with those weird buttons in your earlobes, unless you want to work at an organic grocery store or indie coffee shop but then why am I paying for college?-EMILY Mom, slow down. I'm not gonna do anything crazy. I'm just going to write my songs and join an a cappella group.
Not just any a cappella group. Ignore what Lester Holt said, the Bellas are an institution. My days
Were the best of your life, I know. And I can't wait to be one -unless they don't take me?
Oh, they'll take you. Thanks to me, you were born into it. (holds her cheeks) Then I'll be your mother and your sister.
Gross. Okay -Emily looks over at the church and takes a deep breath.
And the next phase of my life...
Emily takes a big, symbolic step away from Katherine.
Begins! (then, to a PASSERBY) Hi. I'm an adult now. (to another PASSERBY) I can't wait to exchange ideas with
Emily! Could you be any more embarrassing?! (then, loudly singing)
OOH-OOH-OOH BABY PLEASE DON'T GO! Emily sweetly waves good-bye at her teary-eyed mother and enters the church.
INT. DEAN OF STUDENT AFFAIRS OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
The Bellas still stand before the Dean, Gail, and John.
Hold on... Wait a second...
A long beat. Then, the Dean whispers to John and Gail.
How long are we going to wait?
I got all day./We don't do much.
SHOOTING DRAFTS
(holds up file) There's nothing in here that strips us of our I.C.C.A. title. And if we're still reigning champs... then we're automatically invited to represent America at the World
Championships this Spring. Eyes wide, the others look to each other.

(commentator voice) Ah, yes. The World Championships of A Cappella. Where every four years, the best from all over the globe compete for world domination. (withering a little) I'm sorry, I feel like I have to be "on" all the time or people won't like me.
So this championship is like the Olympics?
Actually, a cappella competitions started the year before the first games in Athens. The discus was invented because they wanted something to throw at the singers.
(aside to Chloe) Is that true?
It might be. I'm failing history, too.
Good luck paying those student loans, Chlo.
Look, ladies. We can't keep you from World's competition, but it's not going to help your case here.
Not at all. What if we win it?
The Bellas perk up. John and Gail laugh, hard.
What if you win it? What if you win it? Like, you outperform the other groups. Ho ho! How do you fit such big dreams in such a small body? What if you win it?
The two laugh even harder.
What if you win it?
Yeah. If we win it, will you reinstate us?
(through laughter) Sure. But no American team has ever won.
(proudly) That's because they hate us.
The whole world.
(through laughter) The whole world hates us! Off their continued laughter...
INT. CHURCH - MOMENTS LATER
Emily sits among the FRESHMAN CLASS in a beautiful, old cathedral. An enthusiastic orientation REPRESENTATIVE addresses the room flanked by Barden cheerleaders.
Hello, fresh persons! Welcome to Barden University!
A couple MALE CHEERLEADERS shoot off T-Shirt cannons. The Barden Knight mascot runs wildly across the stage.
You have made a great choice. And a cheap one. And while our football team did not crack the top hundred, we did climb to number three in the nation for HPV cases.

Emily takes notes. ANGLE ON: Her notepad. She writes "HPV = Everywhere." Above it, there's a to-do list: "Buy shower shoes. Call Mom. Audition for Bellas." On the other page, she's written dozens of song lyrics.
We have a special performance for you now. Here they are, the pride of Barden University -(reading off card) The Trey blem-akers!
Our TREBLEMAKERS run in. Jesse addresses the room.
Thank you! We are the Treblemakers. (off Rep's shrug) You can catch us every Wednesday
night outside the Science Center
They sing Mika's "Lollipop."
WHAT'S THE BIG IDEA?/YO, MIKA
I SAID SUCKING TOO HARD ON YOUR
LOLLIPOP/OH, LOVES GONNA GET YOU

YOUR LOLLIPOP/OH, LOVES GONNA GET YOU DOWN/SUCKING TOO HARD ON YOUR LOLLIPOP/LOVES GONNA GET YOU DOWN/ SUCKING TOO HARD ON YOUR LOLLIPOP LOVES GONNA GET YOU DOWN/SAY LOVE, SAY LOVE/LOVES GONNA GET YOU DOWN SAY LOVE, SAY LOVE... As the Trebles sing, they perform a fun choreographed routine. It's classic Trebles. A couple of GIRLS swoon over Jesse. Emily reacts, impressed.
...SAY LOVE, SAY LOVE/OH LOVES
SHOOTING DRAFTS They land their final pose and bow. The Rep takes the stage.
EXT. BARDEN CAMPUS - CHURCH - DAY
The Trebles stand next to a table marked "WELCOME PACKETS & HPV VACCINES." Emily walks up to grab her welcome packet.
Great job, Trebles. You killed it. Jesse and Benji turn to Emily. Benji is immediately smitten.
Thanks. You like a cappella?
Oh yeah. I got my heart set on becoming a Bella. It's at the very top of all my dream boards.
Benji, nervous, steps in front of Jesse and offers his hand.
Did I hear mention of dreams? Hi. Benjamin Applebaum at your service. May I say, you are so spirited. I just want to put you in a box and
saw you in half -Jesse PULLS Benji back.
For magic! As part of a trick. He does magic. It's only weird if you don't embrace it.
An amused Emily crosses off. Jesse turns to Benji. SHOOTING DRAFTS
Dude. Explain yourself.
I completely blacked out. How'd I do, man?
Honestly? I've seen you do worse.
EXT. DEAN OF STUDENT AFFAIRS OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
Chloe bursts out the doors followed by the Bellas.
Oh my god oh my god oh my god...
Chloe, don't blame yourself. You're a ginger, that's punishment enough. This is not all your fault. This is on all of us.
No it's my fault. If only I tamed my beast more like I say I'm gonna do every New Year's.
So if we don't win the World's, what are we? Just a bunch of girls who hang out?
What's wrong with that?
Everything! I won't let us go down in history as the team that served a clam pie to the first family! BECA (snide, to Lilly) Imagine the headlines if Bush had been president.
"Meek Co-ed Seduces Dick Cheney."
Beca shoots her a confused look. Beca's cell buzzes. She steps away to answer it. Chloe addresses the others.
This is the biggest challenge that any of us have ever faced!
When I was nine, my brother tried to sell me for a chicken. So....
Well, I will do whoever it takes to get to the top.
You mean, "whatever" it takes.
Yeah, I'll do that, too.
ANGLE ON: An excited Beca, quietly talking on her phone.

That's great-- Yeah, I can start Monday. I would be stoked to start on Monday. Sorry, I don't say stoked. I don't say stuff like that. I'm pretty cool. You're gonna like me. (winces) That was bad. Back with the others,
We just need to attack this problem head on. With a hundred percent commitment and laser focus. Right? (into phone) Great. I will see you on Monday. Beca OUT! (hangs up) What?
Yeah.
Beca runs back into the Bella circle for a hands in and we
"Pitch Perfect 2" EXT. ELECTRIC PONY RECORDING STUDIO - CURBSIDE - DAYS LATER 18
ESTABLISHING SHOT. Jesse's car pulls into frame.
INT. CAR/EXT. ELECTRIC PONY RECORDING STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
Jesse and Beca pull up in Jesse's car outside the main entrance of a hip-looking recording studio.
Lil Wayne's recorded here, huh? Yep. And was arrested here twice. (then) Can you believe they hired me?
Of course I can. You rock the party that rocks the body.
I don't... know... where to begin with that.
They both exit the car. Jesse walks around to Beca who pulls her computer bag from the back seat.
So... Any first day jitters?
(a little cocky) Nah, you know me. I'm just gonna act all moody and distant. Artists love it.
You're all so tortured inside. Try buying a kitten.
You don't buy kittens. You can get them free, literally, anywhere.
There it is! That's what hooked me!
Beca cracks a smile.
Dude, why do I feel so guilty about taking this internship? (MORE)
I've given a lot to the Bellas, like three years of my life.
Yeah, Bec, you shouldn't feel guilty for taking your shot. This is a big deal, right?
Yes. This is a very big deal.
Beca kisses Jesse goodbye and heads inside. He shouts after her.
NOTHIN' GONNA STOP MY GIRL!
Please don't embarrass me.
(laughing) You can go now!
Beca turns the corner of the building. Jesse gets back in the car and drives off.
EXT. BELLA HOUSE - LATER
ESTABLISHING SHOT of the Bella house, a nice two-story colonial. It's currently toilet-papered and egged.
INT. BELLA HOUSE - DAY
At the kitchen table, Fat Amy, Flo and Stacie go through a huge stack of letters. Chloe is busy on her laptop.
It's been months. We're still getting hate mail?
You'd think my vagina beat up Rihanna.
Chloe hits a button on the computer.
Okay, we're officially registered. Update your passports, ladies. 'Cause we're headed to the very sunny, very beautiful...
They all jump up and down, excited.
Yeah! Where is that?
No idea! I failed Maps!
How is that a course?!
(off computer) Looks like it's been dominated by that stupid German team that took over our tour.
You mean stole our tour. Word. We need to scout those schweinhunds.
Fat Amy picks up one of the letters.
But I'm not worried. When we win, we can stick it to these chumps who sent us all this hate mail, like -(off letter) Sonia Sotomayor. REVEAL: The letter says "DIE BITCHES" in big, black letters.
Judge-y bitch. The Bellas are back!
Where's Beca? She's missing all the fun!
Fat Amy shrugs as we CUT TO: "Pitch Perfect 2"
INT. EP RECORDING STUDIO - HALLWAY/BULLPEN - DAY

FLASH! Beca, disoriented by a flashbulb, gets her picture taken. THE SAME PICTURE is hung around her neck on a big green ID Badge. IN QUICK SUCCESSION: She POPS coffee pods into three different machines lined up on a kitchen counter and hits three different brew buttons. She grabs a bag of chips and holds it with her mouth. Then, grabs several coffees. Beca walks briskly through a super hip office, gold and platinum records lining the walls. She takes a wrong turn, corrects herself, and finally lands in a bullpen. She hands out coffee to HIPSTER EMPLOYEES who don't even look at her. A hipster employee, DAX, talks on the phone and SNATCHES the chips right out of her mouth.

Thanks. I was having a hard time breathing. Anything else I -Dax impatiently WAVES her away. Beca nods "got it." Then, SAMMY, 40s, a music producer who is always wearing designer t-shirts, sunglasses and a ball cap, sweeps in.
Okay, everybody huddle up. Let's go! Let's go! To the table please!

A dozen EMPLOYEES from all over the studio begin crowding into the bullpen. Following their lead, Beca DRAGS a chair over. An IN-CHARGE WOMAN smiles and takes it, assuming Beca brought it for her. Beca awkwardly sits on the floor.
Come on. Last one in has to help Frank watch Youtube videos to find the next Justin Bieber.
PAN OVER to a really young millenial, FRANK.
Hey, I'm turning twenty-five next week if anyone wants to get drinks, celebrate...
SHOOTING DRAFTS
Frank, less talk. More watching kids in their bathrooms play
(then) Okay, my people. Check it.
Sammy PUSHES a button on a remote. On a projection screen, CALVIN "SNOOP LION" BROADUS, in a three-piece suit, stands at a candlelit gathering, surrounded by MULTI-CULTURAL CHILDREN. That is --
The screen stops working. A frustrated Sammy turns to Dax.
Dax, did you call the tech guy?
Yeah I talked to him.
(shakes head) Do you understand that everything else in my life works? Uh huh.
So I just need everything in here to work, too.
He said he was gonna call me back -Oh my god.
You want me to call him now?
(beyond frustrated) Don't... you do it now.
(suddenly nervous)
Sammy hits a button on the remote.
There we go. (puts remote down)
That is the lion himself, legendary Snoop D-O-Double-G singing "White Christmas" at a tree lighting ceremony in Moscow. They all react, "nice."

He was so moved by the power of music to unite the world or some shit that now he wants to drop a cool Christmas album, put his spin on the classics. And because I sleep on a bed of Grammys, he's hired me to produce it.
There's a murmur of excitement.
But Snoop Dogg already put out a

If you'd listened to it like I did -stranded in the air with T.I. on a golden hang glider, then you would have known that it wasn't the classics. I've had to listen to that album on two different occasions... Hang glider with T.I. and also on a rocket ship that Eminem built. It doesn't go anywhere, but he's got dreams for it, okay? So I need you to close your mouth. (then, re: screen) Herein lies the problem. Last I checked there are over a million
ten damn songs on them. So we need all hands on deck to come up with ideas on how to make this one stand out. You got me? Fire when ready.
The Employees all look at each other. After a beat,
What if we get those dogs that bark jingle bells to back him up?
Sammy tries to stay composed.
Let me guess. You want those dogs to back him up because his name is Snoop Dogg? Yeah!
(to everyone else) That was a really great example of a horrible idea. Take a lap. What?
Take a lap. But I'm wearing skinny jeans.
I don't care. Go, go, go. I want to see knees up.
As everyone watches, Dax struggles to jog around the studio.
Knees up please! (then) Guys, I gonna give you a little more time. A minute. One minute.
Sixty seconds.
Beca reacts, "gulp." As Sammy exits,
My time is like a toddler in a tiara -- precious and short. Good ideas, my people! Snoop is coming!
Sammy leaves.
INT. BELLA HOUSE - NIGHT
SFX: A DOOR BELL.
EXT. BELLA HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
Emily stands on the porch. Fat Amy answers.
Sorry my boobs are all crazy. I was
just jumping.
Hi, I just came from auditions. You guys weren't there, and I was hoping for the chance to sing for you.
No. Can't help you. We're not allowed to take anyone new.
Fat Amy goes to shut the door but Emily squeezes her way in.
INT. BELLA HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
No, no, wait! I'm a Junk!
Cynthia Rose crosses by the door.
What you say about your junk?

(nervous energy) Hi. I'm Emily Junk. I know, it's weird. It's my mom's last name. My dad's last name is Hardon, so as a family, we just decided to go with Junk. Originally it was pronounced Yunk but then my great grandfather had a falling out with his brother
and right before he shot him he-Emily stops when she notices the Bellas, staring agape.
I'm a legacy. Junk. My mom was a Bella.
(realizing, gasps) Your mother is Katherine Junk? Who?
Only the top bitch of the 1981 Bellas. (MORE)
She pioneered the syncopated booty shake. And word is she had a fiveoctave vocal range.
Still does. You do not want to hear that woman doing it with my dad. FAT AMY What an odd thing to say.
Jessica and Ashley enter.
Look, everyone knows you're supposed to make room for legacies. It's what makes this country fair and great.
Not its abundant clean drinking water?
True. If a legacy wants to audition we have to let her. (as she sits) Okay, show us what you got.
Here. Right here. Right now.
All the ladies take a seat and face Emily, who is suddenly very nervous.

Okay. I'd like to perform an original song I'm working on. I'm not finished with it so... let's not be dicks about it. (beat) Sorry, was that too crass? It was. (then) Fat Amy, you have a lovely vagina.
Thank you. Proceed.
Emily sings her original song "Flashlight." Her eyes are always closed. Emily has a soft, lovely voice.
OWN/FEELING FRIGHTENED OF THE THINGS THAT I DON'T KNOW/WHEN TOMORROW COMES/WHEN TOMORROW COMES/WHEN TOMORROW COMES... Emily's soft voice draws the Bellas in closer.
AND THOUGH THE ROAD IS LONG-(opens eyes)
(then, singing the chorus) I/'CAUSE I LOOK AROUND ME/AND SEE A DARK/BUT YOU'RE MY FLASHLIGHT/ FLASHLIGHT... YOU'RE MY FLASHLIGHT. A beat.
Would you excuse us a moment?
The Bellas huddle up and speak in hushed tones.
She's pretty good. Can we take her?
What do you expect us to say? She's standing right there. (then, "discreetly") Definitely not a soloist.
